channel.title: Comments for page "Win Condition"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Win Condition"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No voted. The "fix history" concept is relatively played out to my mind (should I have worked out who was doing so here?), and while the writing was solid, I never felt completely engaged by the characters (and never quite joined the dots between them all). My favourite part by far is the idea that the Salem witch trials were Foundation work - that is very neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this has something to do with the 001 Proposal by Swann, and someone within the foundation is making the author of this make sure the foundation survives to keep the world from being destroyed by 682 for example. Whether this was intentional or not I cannot say, but it is genius. +1s all around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On one hand, I like the concept of Dead Hand punching a hole through a pocket dimension on its way to do its thing, and causing all sorts of problems along the way, but none of the sections with Charles, Edward, etc, really moved me or pulled me in, even as the sections were being revised and rewritten by the anomaly. I'm going to err on the side of a no-vote. I really like the concept, but the execution here doesn't really do much for me as a reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another entry playing with the entire notion of "history". I'm not sure I really know what's going on, but yet I never felt lost. "could ever possibly forget" is a very unfortunate typo, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to ChaoSera for the German translation, and related advise on phrasing!
Thanks to Roget for proposing the layout of the letter and its translation!
Thanks to Athenodora for inspiring me with the last scene and thus, completing the story!
Edit: Removed Three Portland link upon elaboration from GreenWolf.
